name: Blackledge Country Club
about: Blackledge Country Club is a 36-hole public golf facility located in Hebron, Connecticut, featuring two courses: Anderson's Glen and Gilead Highlands. The venue provides an enjoyable challenge for players of all abilities with its lush rolling fairways, scenic views, and carefully manicured greens. During the off-season, the facility operates three HD Golf Simulators and its Blackledge River Tavern, which is open 7 days a week and offers weekend entertainment and dinner specials. Blackledge is also available for corporate tournaments, fundraising events, and golf groups of any size.
why_golfers_like_it: 3 HD Golf Simulators for year-round play
Rated #7 nationally by Golf Advisor for course conditions
Full-service tavern with weekend entertainment and dining specials
Hosts corporate tournaments and private events of all sizes
Comprehensive practice facility with instruction available

food_and_drink.notes: The Blackledge River Tavern offers a full dining experience, voted 'Reader's Choice Best Restaurant in Tolland County' by Connecticut Magazine. Available for private parties and special events.
Full bar service available at the Tavern, with a dedicated 'Now on Tap!' section featuring draft beers.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: While you can bring your own clubs, the facility likely provides rental options as it's a full-service golf facility. The pro shop would have club rentals available.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The Blackledge River Tavern offers full food and beverage service, so external food and drinks are likely not permitted inside the facility.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: Standard 18-hole simulator rounds typically take 1-2 hours depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is Blackledge Country Club kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Blackledge is family-friendly and offers a welcoming environment for juniors and youth golfers, as indicated by their family-oriented facilities and services.
